mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate directly raises the discount rate for equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-increase periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital crosses a structural threshold that triggers rapid portfolio de-risking and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses the 4.5% threshold, or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for yield curve slope.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and expansion in total nonfarm payrolls confirms robust labor market absorption, driving consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations higher, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and housing starts indicate strengthening household balance sheets and durable goods demand, which translates into higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show month-over-month growth, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which mechanically supports near-term equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day forward returns in >65% of observations, or the point-biserial correlation between INDPRO changes and direction_20d falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds the 200 threshold, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting anticipatory monetary tightening and margin compression that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL breaches 200, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for nominal interest rates and real wage growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with accelerating consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real discount rates and corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic normalization, a declining unemployment rate signals improving labor market efficiency and rising wage growth, which boosts consumer spending forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of observations during normalization phases, or the predictive power vanishes when UNRATE falls below 4.0%.
